hawkish_phrases: Two members preferred to increase the target for the stock of asset purchases by an additional £100 billion at this meeting
the Committee judges that the existing stance of monetary policy is appropriate
illustrative scenario...inflation rises to around the 2% target
dovish_phrases: Activity has fallen sharply since the beginning of the year and unemployment has risen markedly
unprecedented falls during late March and early April
CPI inflation declined to 1.5% in March and is likely to fall below 1% in the next few months
very sharp fall in UK GDP in 2020 H1 and a substantial increase in unemployment
the balance of risks to the economic outlook lies to the downside
summary: The MPC unanimously maintained Bank Rate at 0.1% and voted 7-2 to continue the £200 billion asset purchase programme (two members wanted an additional £100 billion), with extreme dovish bias reflecting unprecedented economic collapse, sharply rising unemployment, and downside risks dominating in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic.
